_INSTRUCTIONS_FOR_ROUTINE_AGENT: ROUTINE REVIEW AGENT — READ THIS FIRST. This file controls Question 2 of the in-app user feedback flow. Q1 (satisfaction emoji scale) and Q3 (optional free-text) are hard-coded in Views/FeedbackSheet.swift and you MUST NOT touch them. Q2 is yours: edit `question_text`, `type`, and (when multiple_choice) `options`. The app reads this file on every Idle-screen visit, so the next user prompt picks up your changes after a rebuild (`swift run`). User responses are written to ~/Library/Application Support/DynamicPomodoro/feedback.json — that's the file you read to deliberate improvements. SCHEMA: `question_text` (string, ≤80 chars), `type` ("multiple_choice" | "open_ended"), `options` (array of 2–5 strings ≤24 chars each, REQUIRED for multiple_choice, OMIT for open_ended). Bump `_revision` every time you change the question so responses can be traced to a version. The Swift decoder is strict — do not add or remove top-level fields. WORDING RULE: most features in this app have no agreed user-facing name. When asking about one, identify it by what the user sees (e.g. "the short italic line shown during breaks"), name the screen/stage where it appears, and quote a short example if it's text — never use internal code names like "reminder quote" or "skip nudge". If you can't fit that in 80 chars, the question is too narrow; widen the scope. See Resources/AGENT_README.md (same folder) for the longer write-up.
question_text: Do the breaks actually help you refocus?
